title: Pseudo API client for fetching and searching .pseudo files
purpose: Provides HTTP methods to fetch .pseudo file contents, search across files, and resolve symbol references from a project.
module_context: Exports type definitions for pseudo file structures and a set of async fetch functions for interacting with a pseudo API backend.

steps.content: Encode the project name for use in a URL query parameter.
Send a GET request to the pseudo files endpoint with the encoded project.
IF the response is not successful, throw an error with the status text.
Parse the JSON response and return the array of file summaries.
IF parsing fails or the data is malformed, re-throw the error.
Encode the project and file names for use in a URL query parameter.
Send a GET request to the pseudo file endpoint with the encoded parameters.
IF the response is not successful, throw an error with the status text.
Parse the JSON response and return the file with methods data.
IF parsing fails or the data is malformed, re-throw the error.

methods.name: fetchPseudoReferences
methods.normalized_params: (project: string, functionName: string, fileStem: string): Promise<Reference[]>

steps.content: Construct a URL search parameter string with project, function name, and file stem.
Send a GET request to the references endpoint with the constructed parameters.
IF the response is not successful, throw an error with the status text.
Parse the JSON response and return the array of reference objects.
IF parsing fails or the data is malformed, return an empty array.
Encode the project and query strings for use in a URL query parameter.
Send a GET request to the search endpoint with the encoded parameters.
IF the response is not successful, throw an error with the status text.
Parse the JSON response and return the array of search results.
IF parsing fails or the data is malformed, re-throw the error.
Construct a URL search parameter string with project and source path.
Send a GET request to the functions for source endpoint with the constructed parameters.
IF the response is not successful, throw an error with the status text.
Parse the JSON response and return the array of function objects.
IF parsing fails or the data is malformed, return an empty array.

methods.name: fetchSourceLink
methods.normalized_params: (project: string, name: string, hintFileStem?: string): Promise<SourceLinkCandidate[]>

steps.content: Construct a URL search parameter string with project and name.
IF a hint file stem is provided, add it to the search parameters.
Send a GET request to the source link endpoint with the constructed parameters.
IF the response is not successful, throw an error with the status text.
Parse the JSON response and return the array of source link candidates.
IF parsing fails or the data is malformed, return an empty array.
